steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Start with $$(7x^3+7x)-(6x^3-3x).$$ Change the signs in the second parentheses:
$$7x^3+7x-6x^3+3x.$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: The cubic terms give $7x^3-6x^3=x^3$. The linear terms give $7x+3x=10x$. So the expression is $$x^3+10x.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The correct answer is exactly $x^3+10x$. The other choices have the wrong cubic coefficient, and a choice with the wrong linear coefficient. Therefore A is correct.
